subject: [PATCH v3 09/10] fuse: add zero-copy over io-uring

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about unregistration of io-uring buffers during the abort race, explaining that it requires nontrivial changes to io-uring and would introduce complexity. They acknowledge that leaving the buffer table slot occupied until the io-uring ring is destroyed is acceptable.
reviews.raw_body:
Revisiting this... I had this cleanup in the series I sent out last
December [1] but in order to accommodate unregistration on teardown
during the abort race, it requires nontrivial changes to io-uring, as
it requires operating on a ring ctx handle but it's tricky to
ascertain if the handle is valid or not. I had discussed it with Jens
at LPC last year, and we came to the conclusion that it's not worth
introducing the complexity to accommodate this specific scenario and
it's fine to leave the buffer table slot occupied with pinned folios
until the io-uring ring is destroyed.

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/io-uring/20251203003526.2889477-30-joannelkoong@gmail.com/

2026-06-04.patch_summary: This patch adds zero-copy support to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) io-uring implementation, allowing for direct access to client pages or page cache folios without intermediate memory copies. The kernel registers relevant pages into a sparse slot in the server's io_uring registered buffer table, enabling the server to operate on these pages directly using fixed buffer operations. This feature is gated behind CAP_SYS_ADMIN and FUSE_URING_REGISTERED_BUFFERS, requiring bufpools and keeping configuration space small. Zero-copy allows the server direct access to client pages, eliminating the need for intermediary buffers or page cache folios.
